query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's my summary of findings on PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan's ADIZ in May–June 2026:

## Key Findings

**Overall trajectory — sustained low in 2026:** PLA air activity around Taiwan has been notably lower throughout 2026 than in 2024–2025. Every month from January through May 2026 was the lowest for its corresponding month since January 2023. A distinctive feature is that air activity fell while naval (PLAN) activity rose to record levels.

**The reported "pause"/drop:**
- A ~one-week complete pause occurred **February 27 – March 5, 2026**, with **zero PLA aircraft** near the ADIZ.
- The Taiwan Security Monitor (GMU) concluded this most likely reflected routine cyclical variation (NPC "Two Sessions" + Spring Festival), not strategic signaling. Reuters noted a possible Trump–Xi meeting factor.
- The broader 2026 decline is year-long, attributed partly to PLA internal purges and training reforms.

**Monthly figures:**
- March 2026: 171 total sorties (lowest month since the Aug 2022 post-Pelosi surge)
- May 2026: no single day exceeded 30 sorties
- **June 2026: 214 total sorties, 134 median-line crossings** — well below June 2024/2025

**Typical recent daily counts:** Mostly **2–10 sorties per day** in late May through July 2026, with occasional patrol/long-range-training days reaching ~22. This is a clear step-down from the 30+ sortie days and periodic 50–90+ surges common in 2024–2025.

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** **36 sorties on March 17, 2026** — the year's peak. No day since has exceeded 30. (A larger ~130-aircraft drill occurred Dec 29–30, 2025, but that's outside 2026.)

Full structured findings — including all data points and sources with access dates — have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.snippet: In June 2026, the PLA conducted 134 median line crossings and 214 total aircraft sorties detected around Taiwan... 225 PLAN vessels detected, highest June total on record.

sources.snippet: January through May 2026 was the lowest for the corresponding month since January 2023... no single day in May recorded more than 30 detected aircraft sorties. Last time daily sorties exceeded 30 was March 17.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From February 27th to March 5th, Taiwan's MND reported no PLA aircraft operating near Taiwan's ADIZ... likely routine cyclical variation driven by domestic political events.

sources.title: March 2026 PLA aircraft sorties around Taiwan (171 total; peak 36 on March 17)
sources.snippet: In March 2026, a total of 171 PLA aircraft sorties were recorded around Taiwan... peak of 36 aircraft recorded on March 17, 2026.

sources.snippet: 10 sorties of PLA aircraft, 8 PLAN ships and 4 official ships operating around Taiwan detected as of 6 a.m.

sources.snippet: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships operating around Taiwan detected as of 6 a.m.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan — July 8, 2026
sources.snippet: 9 sorties of PLA aircraft, 9 PLAN ships and 3 official ships operating around Taiwan detected as of 6 a.m.

sources.title: MND Press Release: PLA Long-Range Flight Training and Joint Combat Readiness Patrol — July 2, 2026
sources.snippet: Starting at 0653 hours, a total of 22 sorties of various PLA combat and auxiliary aircraft (J-16, H-6, KJ-500) detected.

sources.title: 90 Chinese Aircraft Cross Taiwan Strait Median Line in Massive PLA Drill (Dec 2025)
sources.snippet: Late December 2025 drill: 130 aircraft total, 90 crossing the median line; short of the record 153 during 2024 Joint Sword.

sources.title: Chinese military flights around Taiwan fall; Trump-Xi meeting may be a factor
sources.snippet: Referenced re: possible factors in the Feb–Mar 2026 drop in PLA flights.

sources.title: PLA sends 8 vessels, 24 aircraft across median line
sources.snippet: Eight Chinese naval vessels and 24 military aircraft detected crossing the median line (March 17–18, 2026).

key_facts: June 2026: 214 total PLA aircraft sorties detected around Taiwan and 134 median-line crossings — substantially below June 2024 and June 2025 (K. Tristan Tang / Taiwan MND data).
Every month January through May 2026 recorded the lowest PLA aircraft sorties for the corresponding month since January 2023.
Highest single-day PLA aircraft count in 2026: 36 sorties on March 17, 2026 (peak for the year).
As of the May 2026 update, no single day in May exceeded 30 sorties, and the last day sorties exceeded 30 was March 17, 2026.
A ~one-week complete pause occurred February 27 – March 5, 2026, with ZERO PLA aircraft reported near the ADIZ — described as an unusually long pause.
Taiwan Security Monitor concluded the Feb–Mar 2026 pause most plausibly reflected routine cyclical variation (NPC 'Two Sessions' and Spring Festival), not strategic signaling.
March 2026 had 171 total sorties — lowest monthly figure since the post-Pelosi surge began August 2022.
Naval activity diverged upward: 225 PLAN vessels detected in June 2026 — highest June total on record.
Recent daily sortie counts (late May–July 2026) mostly ranged from 2 to ~10, with occasional patrol days ~22, far below 2024–2025 norms.
A larger ~130-aircraft drill (90 median-line crossings) occurred Dec 29–30, 2025, but is outside 2026.
